title: Financial Literacy and Education Commission
summary: The Financial Literacy and Education Commission (FLEC) is a federal interagency body established under the Fair and Accurate Credit Transactions Act of 2003, chaired by the Treasury Secretary and vice-chaired by the CFPB Director, comprising 24 federal agencies. The page documents FLEC's mission to coordinate financial literacy programs, publishes annual SAFE reports to Congress, and lists historical meetings including one on February 6, 2026 featuring a briefing on 'Trump Accounts' implementation.
key_points: FLEC was established under the Fair and Accurate Credit Transactions Act of 2003
Commission comprises heads of 24 federal agencies including Treasury, CFPB, Federal Reserve, SEC, FDIC, OCC, and others
Chaired by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent; Vice-chaired by CFPB Director
Latest public meeting held February 6, 2026 from 10:00 AM – 11:45 AM Eastern Time
Meeting featured briefing on implementation of Trump Accounts and panel on youth financial education
MyMoney.gov serves as the federal government's financial literacy website available in English and Spanish
FLEC publishes annual SAFE (Strategy for Assuring Financial Empowerment) reports to Congress
why_it_matters: This source is not directly relevant to market structure or macro catalysts. It describes a federal consumer education initiative rather than providing market-moving information, monetary policy signals, or economic data. The content is institutional and informational about financial education programs.
